note: Time-indexed model registry (Part 15 Arm A). An event enters Arm A only if a checkpoint with a documented knowledge cutoff BEFORE the event's forecast cutoff exists AND its weights are verifiable. Weight-hash verification is impossible for hosted API models, so Arm A eligibility below is graded, not asserted.

models.model_hash: UNAVAILABLE — hosted API; weights not hash-verifiable
models.release_date: 2024-12-26 (V3); serving version updated by provider over time
models.eligibility: INELIGIBLE for strict Arm A: the served checkpoint mutates (V3->V3-0324->...) and no immutable hash exists, so 'documented training-data cutoff before event cutoff' cannot be PROVEN for the weights actually serving a given request. Recorded as the exact external blocker for Arm A.

arm_a_status: BLOCKED_EXTERNAL: no verifiable pre-cutoff checkpoint is accessible from this environment (requires a pinned open-weights checkpoint, e.g. a dated Llama/Qwen release with published weights + hash, plus inference hardware). Until then only Arm B (causally blinded current model) is a clean arm, and the mandatory 2-clean-arms-per-cutoff requirement CANNOT be satisfied — the 800-forecast benchmark is therefore formally incomplete regardless of row count.
unblock_path: pin an open-weights checkpoint with published hash + documented cutoff (e.g. released before 2024-03), host it, register {model, sha256, release_date, cutoff}, mark eligible events, regenerate Arm A rows.
